title: Buildah CI hard to reproduce locally
body:
The Buildah CI heavily relies on the logic in the .travis file to set up a local registry along with credentials etc. This makes it incredibly hard to reproduce issues locally. At the moment, I ran into the situation where Travis seems to be stuck on a deadlock that I am unable to reproduce locally.

I think it is beneficial to be able to execute and reproduce the integration tests locally (maybe in a container). I guess this work will consume some time but we can use this issue a tracker.

Cc: @TomSweeneyRedHat @rhatdan @cevich @edsantiago

comments.body:
I agree this is a useful thing. Though I would recommend treading carefully with using containers, even if super-privileged.  Experience with `containers/storage` (repo. CI) has shown that it works fine for simple cases.  However, as needs grow deeper/more advanced (including debugging-ability), getting out from a container-setup can be a major headache.  There are simply certain resources inaccessible from containers, and if required for testing, demand a LOT of hoop-jumping.

In any case, some suggested first steps would be:

1. Move the commands from `.travis.yml` into dedicated scripts, then call those from the `yaml`.
2. After that's working, modify on each script, separating items specific to environment setup vs testing into their own scripts.
3. Decide/Define/Document a very specific "Supported Host Environment" for devel. and debugging.  Ideally this should match as closely as possible to the desired CI environment.
4. Make the separated environment-specific script's match the definition.
5. From the top-level scripts, make the ci-environment scripts configurable.  So they can be substituted for other calls when operating outside of Travis/CI.  For example (using `before_install`), maybe have a `before_install_ubuntu.sh` and `before_install_fedora.sh`
5. Forever maintain the set of environment scripts, such that they remain as identical and reproducible as possible.
Is anyone working on this?  Should we just break out the tests that rely on registries into a separate PR.  Is the move to Cirrus going to make this any easier?

I believe migrating from travis to cirrus-ci + a functional 'hack/get_ci_vm.sh' should take care of this.  Granted it doesn't completely fix the "reproduce locally" issue, but better codifies the environment in a reusable way.
As soon as we create the new Release we can merge this.  Please hold off until then.
We are now using cirrus, is that enough to close this issue?
I'm making the executive decision to close this given travis is no longer in the picture.  @vrothberg please reopen or create a new issue if you feel otherwise.
